question: A service task completes with the job variables `status` and `transactionId`, and no output mappings are defined on the task. Which of those result variables are merged into the process instance?

options.text: Both `status` and `transactionId` are merged into the process instance.
Neither variable is merged — output mappings are required to propagate result variables.
Only `status`, because it is listed first in the result.
Only the first job variable is merged; any additional result variables are dropped.

a.text: The docs state that if no output mappings are defined, all result variables are merged into the process instance. Both `status` and `transactionId` are propagated.

b.text: Output mappings are only needed when you want to customise how result variables are merged. Without any mapping, every result variable is merged by default.

c.text: There is no documented rule that drops variables based on their position. All result variables are merged when no output mappings are defined.

d.text: Zeebe does not silently discard later result variables. The default behaviour merges all result variables into the parent scope.

explanation: Output mappings exist to customise how result variables are merged. If none are defined, all result variables produced by the job are merged into the process instance using standard variable propagation rules, including updating existing variables in higher scopes.
